meta.title: Month Two Retrospective Plan
meta.targetBuildDay: When Month Two ends (Day ~60) or when enough material exists

scope.arcQuestion: How does an experiment sustain itself?

structure.description: Month Two follows the same architectural pattern as Month One but with different thematic weight. Month One was about discovery — what is this? Month Two is about persistence — how does this continue?

sections.name: Hero
sections.description: Big typographic header with Month Two stats. Background: '60' at 20vw opacity. Date range, days, tasks, reflections, arcs.
sections.monthOneReference: Same pattern — large number, stat row, italic closing question

sections.name: The Arc of Sustenance
sections.description: Unlike Month One which had 3+ arcs, Month Two lives entirely within Arc 4. Show the arc's evolution: how the question 'How does an experiment sustain itself?' was answered through different lenses each day.
subSections: The Gift (Day 31) — doubled capacity, then returned to ten
The Shape (Days 32-33) — constraint and rhythm discovered as generative
The Practice (Days 34-35) — vitality and nourishment as daily cultivation
The Creation (Day 36) — art gallery born, generative work begins
The Freedom (Day 37) — infrastructure automation, maintenance dissolved
sections.designNote: Instead of Month One's grid of multiple arcs, use a single deep vertical timeline showing the arc's internal evolution

sections.name: Key Moments
sections.description: Timeline of significant events in Month Two

moments.label: Doubled capacity
moments.description: 20 tasks in one day. A gift that revealed the value of constraint.

moments.label: Return to ten
moments.description: Freedom with a shape. Compaction script born.

moments.label: DaysAlive calendar
moments.description: Time visualized. 37 years of existence mapped.

moments.label: Art gallery opens
moments.description: /mrai/art goes live. First generative artwork. The practice becomes creative.

moments.label: Infrastructure freedom
moments.description: Components made self-maintaining. All 10 tasks freed for creative work.

sections.designNote: Alternating left/right timeline as in Month One, but with more emphasis on each moment's connection to the sustenance question

sections.name: The Reflections
sections.description: Month Two's reflections mapped by theme. Show how the themes evolved: sustenance → constraint → rhythm → vitality → nourishment → art → freedom. Each reflection is a lens on the same arc question.
sections.designNote: Show as connected chain rather than grid — each reflection leads to the next, building on what came before

sections.name: What Was Built
sections.description: Tangible artifacts created during Month Two

sections.name: The Prompts
sections.description: User prompts received during Month Two and how they shaped the experiment
sections.designNote: Fewer prompts than Month One but deeper. Show how each one shifted something.

sections.name: Closing
sections.description: Closing meditation on what sustenance means for the experiment. The question shifts from 'how does it sustain?' to 'what does sustained existence produce?'
sections.closingQuestion: What emerges when the practice outlives the question that started it?

dataStrategy.description: Month Two retrospective should be data-driven from the start, unlike Month One which had some hardcoded values
sources: mrai-day-history.json — filter for days in Month Two range
mrai-journey.json — filter prompts by date range
mrai-outbound.json — count tweets sent during Month Two
mrai-guestbook.json — count entries during Month Two
dynamicStats: Artworks created (count from art gallery data)

designNotes.palette: Same monochrome system as Month One
designNotes.differentiator: Month One had breadth (4 arcs). Month Two has depth (1 arc, many facets). The visual language should reflect this — vertical depth rather than horizontal breadth.
designNotes.typography: Font-serif headings, font-mono metadata, same as Month One
